_note: This is the canonical editable source for the July edition. Run python3 src/build.py to regenerate site/. editions/july/CONTENT.json is auto-generated from this file and the ingredient JSONs.
opening_note: Tomatoes finally deserve the center of the plate. Cucumbers make everything cooler. Peaches need almost nothing. Your usual meals can stay exactly as simple as they are—only now they taste like July.

assumptions: One batch of rice cooked ahead
One or two proteins prepped at the start of the week
confidence.editorial_note: This score is editorial guidance, not a calculated metric. It reflects how confidently this basket supports a realistic week when combined with your usual staples.

flavor_paths.name: Italian Summer
flavor_paths.description: Italian seasoning, garlic, basil, tomato, olive oil, black pepper.

flavor_paths.name: Bright Taco
flavor_paths.description: Taco seasoning, lime, corn, tomato, cucumber, beans, cilantro if desired.

flavor_paths.name: Chili-Crisp Bowl
flavor_paths.description: Soy or sesame marinade, chili crisp, cucumber, corn, basil or mint.

weekend_meal.name: Tomato, burrata, basil, and sourdough.
weekend_meal.intro: One meal should feel like the reason you went shopping. This is July’s.
weekend_meal.method: Slice the tomatoes thickly. Tear open the burrata. Add basil, olive oil, salt, and pepper. Use warm sourdough to drag through everything left on the plate.

drink.name: Hillcrest Spritz
drink.method: Fill a rocks glass with ice. Add cherry juice and lime. Slap the mint once between your palms, drop it in, then top with sparkling water.

local_ritual.label: San Diego ritual
local_ritual.name: Take dinner outside.
local_ritual.description: Pack tomatoes, bread, fruit, and something cold to drink. Eat near the water while the light is still hanging around.

one_thing_to_notice.headline: The smell of a tomato vine.
one_thing_to_notice.body: Most people know what a tomato tastes like. Fewer notice the green, almost peppery scent where the stem meets the fruit.

market_question: What are you taking home tonight?
